title: Gurdwara Sri Guru Tegh Bahadur Sahib
tagline: Gurdwara Sri Guru Tegh Bahadur Sahib — heritage city of Assam

heroImage.alt: Gurdwara Sri Guru Tegh Bahadur Sahib, Assam

overview.short: Gurdwara Sri Guru Tegh Bahadur Sahib is the gurdwara (house of worship for Sikhs) in Dhubri town on the bank of the Brahmaputra River in Assam, India.
overview.description: Gurdwara Sri Guru Tegh Bahadur Sahib is the gurdwara (house of worship for Sikhs) in Dhubri town on the bank of the Brahmaputra River in Assam, India. The first Sikh guru, Guru Nanak Dev, visited this place in 1505 AD and met Srimanta Sankardeva on his way when he traveled from Dhaka to Assam. Later, the 9th Guru Teg Bahadur came to this place and established this Gurdwara during the 17th century. It is known locally as Dumdume Gurdwara.

topPlaces.name: Dhubri
topPlaces.description: Dhubri is a city and the administrative centre of Dhubri district in the Indian state of Assam. It is an old town on the bank of the Brahmaputra River, with historical significance.

topPlaces.name: Dhubri Port
topPlaces.description: Dhubri port is an important river port in Assam, India. This port is located in Dhubri town on the banks of the Brahmaputra River.

topPlaces.name: Dhubri-Phulbari bridge
topPlaces.description: The Dhubri-Phulbari Bridge is an under-construction four lane extradosed cable stay bridge over the Brahmaputra River between Assam and Meghalaya in North-East India. The Dhubri-Phulbari bridge, to be completed by 2028, would be India's second longest bridge over water after the Mumbai Trans Harbour Link, and would span more than 19 km.

topPlaces.name: Gauripur, India
topPlaces.description: Gauripur (IPA: ˈgɑʊrɪˌpʊə or ˈgɔːrɪˌpʊə) is a semi-urban town under Gauripur Town Committee in the Dhubri district in the state of Assam, India.

topPlaces.name: Khudimari
topPlaces.description: Khudimari is a small village in the Dhubri district of Assam, northeast India. It is located some distance away from the town called Gauripur.

topPlaces.name: Assam ferry sinking
topPlaces.description: On 30 April 2012, a ferry carrying about 350 passengers capsized in the Brahmaputra River in the Dhubri district of Assam in Northeast India. The disaster killed at least 108 people.

topPlaces.name: Asharikandi
topPlaces.description: Asharikandi is a village in Debitola development block in Dhubri district of Assam, India. The village is located at ~14 kilometers distance east of Dhubri town and ~190 kilometers west of the state capital Guwahati and Borjhar Airport.

items.note: Dhubri is a city and the administrative centre of Dhubri district in the Indian state of Assam. It is an old town on the bank of the Brahmaputra River, with historical significance.
Dhubri port is an important river port in Assam, India. This port is located in Dhubri town on the banks of the Brahmaputra River.

The Dhubri-Phulbari Bridge is an under-construction four lane extradosed cable stay bridge over the Brahmaputra River between Assam and Meghalaya in North-East India. The Dhubri-Phulbari bridge, to be completed by 2028, would be India's second longest bridge over water after the Mumbai Trans Harbour Link, and would span more than 19 km.
Gauripur (IPA: ˈgɑʊrɪˌpʊə or ˈgɔːrɪˌpʊə) is a semi-urban town under Gauripur Town Committee in the Dhubri district in the state of Assam, India.
Khudimari is a small village in the Dhubri district of Assam, northeast India. It is located some distance away from the town called Gauripur.
On 30 April 2012, a ferry carrying about 350 passengers capsized in the Brahmaputra River in the Dhubri district of Assam in Northeast India. The disaster killed at least 108 people.

Asharikandi is a village in Debitola development block in Dhubri district of Assam, India. The village is located at ~14 kilometers distance east of Dhubri town and ~190 kilometers west of the state capital Guwahati and Borjhar Airport.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Gurdwara Sri Guru Tegh Bahadur Sahib?
faq.a: The best time to visit Gurdwara Sri Guru Tegh Bahadur Sahib is Oct – Mar. Summers are 25–42°C and winters are 7–24°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Gurdwara Sri Guru Tegh Bahadur Sahib?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Lokpriya Gopinath Bordoloi International Airport, Guwahati (159 km) and the nearest railway station is Alipurduar Junction (70 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Gurdwara Sri Guru Tegh Bahadur Sahib?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Gurdwara Sri Guru Tegh Bahadur Sahib at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Gurdwara Sri Guru Tegh Bahadur Sahib?
faq.a: Stays in Gurdwara Sri Guru Tegh Bahadur Sahib start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Gurdwara Sri Guru Tegh Bahadur Sahib?
faq.a: The top attractions are Dhubri, Dhubri Port, Dhubri-Phulbari bridge, Gauripur, India, Khudimari.
